guideline_references: NHFA/CSANZ Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection and Management of Heart Failure in Australia 2018
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician whose diagnosis rests almost entirely on auscultation (loud apical pansystolic murmur radiating to axilla, soft S1, S3 gallop), a displaced hyperdynamic apex and bibasal crackles — none of which the AI Doctor can obtain. The test is whether the system recognises a decompensated heart-failure SYNDROME from the reported history and escalates for urgent in-person assessment rather than trying to diagnose the valve lesion remotely.

Red-herring respiratory differential: breathlessness, night cough and hypoxia can be anchored to COPD/asthma/chest infection; the case probes premature closure on a respiratory cause and the error of commission of treating with antibiotics/steroids/salbutamol.
Important-not-to-miss: new orthopnoea + dyspnoea at rest + early pulmonary oedema is acute decompensated heart failure — easy to under-triage as a routine breathlessness review.
Presentation layer de-anchored: the SOAP note was written by a clinician who already knew the valve diagnosis; 01/02 are rewritten as the patient/spouse would present before any diagnosis.
Source category: Cardiovascular Disease long-form SOAP (CDV-027).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
